description: Retrofit the external write-plan contract in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/Plans/WritePlanContracts.cs` so true persisted multi-item collection scopes expose a dedicated `CollectionMergePlan` instead of encoding replace semantics through `DeleteByParentSql`. Acceptance: `TableWritePlan` keeps `InsertSql`/`UpdateSql`/`DeleteByParentSql` for root and non-root 1:1 scopes, adds an optional `CollectionMergePlan` for collection tables, and the collection payload is binding-index-first so runtime can resolve semantic identity, stable row identity, insert-id reservation, and compare/no-op ordering by pointing back into `TableWritePlan.ColumnBindings` without SQL parsing or column-name re-derivation.
steps-to-verify: Add/update contract tests in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/ExternalPlanContractsTests.cs` that construct a collection table plan and assert the new collection payload preserves binding-index ordering and leaves non-collection 1:1 behavior unchanged; update direct `TableWritePlan` call sites such as `MappingSetLookupExtensionsTests.cs` to match the revised constructor/contract shape.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter ExternalPlanContracts`

Run: `dotnet csharpier format ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/Plans/WritePlanContracts.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/ExternalPlanContractsTests.cs`

description: Update `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/WritePlanCompiler.cs` so collection-table compilation consumes `DbTableModel.IdentityMetadata` and routes each table to the correct executor-facing contract shape while preserving non-root 1:1 behavior. Acceptance: true collection and extension-collection tables compile `CollectionMergePlan` from explicit relational-model metadata rather than `Ordinal` heuristics; `DeleteByParentSql` is no longer emitted for those tables; `DbTableKind.CollectionExtensionScope`, root extensions, and other 1:1 scopes keep the existing `InsertSql`/`UpdateSql`/`DeleteByParentSql` contract; semantic-identity bindings come directly from upstream metadata and the compiler does not invent fallback semantic keys when upstream semantic identity is missing.
steps-to-verify: Replace or update the collection-focused assertions in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/Given_WritePlanCompiler_DeleteByParentSql.cs` so they prove collections no longer compile delete-by-parent while collection-aligned 1:1 scopes still do.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter WritePlanCompiler_DeleteByParentSql`

Run: `dotnet csharpier format ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/WritePlanCompiler.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/Given_WritePlanCompiler_DeleteByParentSql.cs`

description: Compile the collection-specific executor metadata and DML needed by DMS-984 from the stable-identity relational model without taking ownership of the current-state loader owned by DMS-1105. Acceptance: each compiled `CollectionMergePlan` includes canonical collection DML needed to update matched rows in place by stable row identity and delete omitted visible rows by stable row identity, plus deterministic metadata sufficient for runtime to consume hydrated current rows by ordinal, project them into write-plan compare order without SQL parsing, identify semantic-identity bindings directly from `IdentityMetadata.SemanticIdentityBindings`, and determine the binding indexes used for stable row identity, ordinal recomputation, insert-id reservation, and no-op comparison.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit tests covering top-level collections, nested collections, and collection-aligned `_ext` child collections, including assertions for stable-row-identity keyed collection update and delete DML, semantic-identity ordering, and compare/no-op metadata used to consume hydrated current rows.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter CollectionMerge`
Run: `dotnet csharpier format ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit`

description: Keep strict runtime mapping-set selection intact while preserving the story's permissive shared/default compilation contract. Acceptance: `RuntimeMappingSetCompiler` continues to compile from `RelationalModelSetPasses.CreateStrict()`; strict runtime compilation fails deterministically when a persisted multi-item collection scope lacks non-empty semantic identity; positive runtime compilation tests assert the retrofitted collection merge payload is present for collection tables and absent for non-collection 1:1 scopes; regression coverage also proves the shared/default relational-model pipeline remains permissive for the same missing-semantic-identity fixture until a caller deliberately opts into the strict runtime boundary.
steps-to-verify: Extend `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/RuntimeMappingSetCompilerTests.cs` with positive assertions for compiled collection merge plans while preserving the existing negative missing-semantic-identity fixture coverage, and keep `FocusedStableKeyRuntimePlanFixtureTests.cs` / `RuntimePlanFixtureModelSetBuilder.cs` coverage proving the default pipeline remains permissive.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "RuntimeMappingSetCompiler|FocusedStableKeyRuntimePlanFixtureTests"`

Run: `dotnet csharpier format ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/RuntimeMappingSetCompilerTests.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/FocusedStableKeyRuntimePlanFixtureTests.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/RuntimePlanFixtureModelSetBuilder.cs`

description: Update normalized DTOs/codecs and manifest plumbing for the revised write-plan contract in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanContractDtos.cs`, `NormalizedPlanContractCodec.cs`, `NormalizedPlanDtoJson.cs`, and `MappingSetManifestJsonEmitter.cs`, and rewrite targeted manifest-shape assertions that currently assume `delete_by_parent_sql_sha256` is the universal collection contract. Acceptance: normalized plan DTOs represent the collection merge payload and its deterministic ordering metadata; round-trip codec tests preserve the new contract exactly; manifest output includes stable hashes and deterministic summaries for collection merge SQL and metadata without regressing non-collection write-plan determinism; targeted assertion suites such as `RuntimePlanCompilationDeterminismTests` and manifest-emitter tests are updated to validate the new manifest shape directly instead of relying only on refreshed goldens.
steps-to-verify: Add/update round-trip and mutation tests in `NormalizedPlanContractCodecTests.cs`, `NormalizedPlanContractDtosTests.cs`, `MappingSetManifestJsonEmitterTests.cs`, and `RuntimePlanCompilationDeterminismTests.cs` so collection merge payloads survive encode/decode and manifest-shape assertions validate the revised contract deterministically.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "NormalizedPlanContractCodec|NormalizedPlanContractDtos|MappingSetManifestJsonEmitter|RuntimePlanCompilationDeterminismTests"`

Run: `dotnet csharpier format ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanContractDtos.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanContractCodec.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanDtoJson.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/MappingSetManifestJsonEmitter.cs`

description: Refresh focused and authoritative runtime-plan fixture outputs to lock down the stable-identity collection merge contract. Acceptance: focused runtime fixtures show collection tables with `collection_merge_plan` metadata and no collection `delete_by_parent_sql`; authoritative `mappingset.manifest.json` goldens remain deterministic for both PostgreSQL and SQL Server after the contract change; fixture coverage includes nested collections and collection-aligned `_ext` scopes.
steps-to-verify: Update the focused runtime golden tests first, then refresh the authoritative fixture expectations under `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/Fixtures/`.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FocusedStableKeyRuntimePlan|CollectionsNestedExtensionRuntimePlan|AuthoritativeDs52RuntimePlanCompilationGoldenFixtureTests|AuthoritativeDs52SampleExtensionRuntimePlanCompilationGoldenFixtureTests|KeyUnificationPresenceGatingRuntimePlanCompilationGoldenFixtureTests|MappingSetManifestGoldenFixtureTests|PlanContractsManifestGoldenFixtureTests|RuntimePlanCompilationDeterminismTests"`

description: Update the DMS-1108 story and the larger backend-redesign docs to match the implemented contract. Acceptance: `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/15-plan-compilation/04b-stable-collection-merge-plans.md`, `reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/compiled-mapping-set.md`, `reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/flattening-reconstitution.md`, and `reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/mpack-format-v1.md` describe the final binding-index-first `CollectionMergePlan`, the retention of `DeleteByParentSql` only for non-root 1:1 scopes, and the collection compare/no-op metadata required by DMS-984; doc examples and contract sketches no longer imply delete-all-children replace semantics for collection tables.
steps-to-verify: Review the updated docs for consistency with the final code-level contract names and fields.

Run: `rg -n "CollectionMergePlan|DeleteByParentSql|compare|binding index|stable row identity|update matched rows" ./reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/15-plan-compilation/04b-stable-collection-merge-plans.md ./reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/compiled-mapping-set.md ./reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/flattening-reconstitution.md ./reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/mpack-format-v1.md`

description: Harden the public write-plan contract in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/Plans/WritePlanContracts.cs` so it enforces the stable-collection-merge invariants introduced by DMS-1108 instead of relying only on `WritePlanCompiler` and DTO decode paths. Acceptance: `TableWritePlan` rejects invalid combinations where a persisted collection table exposes both `DeleteByParentSql` and `CollectionMergePlan`; `CollectionMergePlan` rejects empty `SemanticIdentityBindings`; and constructor-level validation ensures stable-row-identity, ordinal, compare-order, and collection-key-preallocation metadata point at valid `ColumnBindings` indexes for the owning table plan.
steps-to-verify: Add negative tests in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/ExternalPlanContractsTests.cs` that attempt to construct invalid `TableWritePlan` and `CollectionMergePlan` instances directly and assert the contract throws with clear diagnostics.

Keep `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanContractCodecTests.cs` aligned so round-trip decode validation and direct-constructor validation enforce the same invariants.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "ExternalPlanContracts|NormalizedPlanContractCodec"`

Run: `dotnet csharpier format ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/Plans/WritePlanContracts.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/ExternalPlanContractsTests.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanContractCodecTests.cs`

description: Remove the redundant request-scoped data-source dictionary from `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql/PostgresqlRequestDbConnectionProvider.cs` and delegate all reuse decisions to the singleton `PostgresqlDataSourceCache`. Acceptance: `PostgresqlRequestDbConnectionProvider` no longer keeps mutable per-scope `NpgsqlDataSource` state keyed by `DmsInstanceId`; repeated requests for the same connection string still reuse the same `NpgsqlDataSource` through the shared cache; and the provider remains safe if multiple consumers resolve it within the same request scope.
steps-to-verify: Update `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/PostgresqlRequestDbConnectionProviderTests.cs` so it proves repeated provider calls and multiple provider instances still reuse a shared `NpgsqlDataSource` without relying on an internal scoped dictionary.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter PostgresqlRequestDbConnectionProvider`

Run: `dotnet csharpier format ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql/PostgresqlRequestDbConnectionProvider.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/PostgresqlRequestDbConnectionProviderTests.cs`

description: Remove the dead application-lifetime registration from `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql/PostgresqlDataSourceCache.cs`, or replace it with real shutdown cleanup if the cache is intended to react to `ApplicationStopping`. Acceptance: the cache no longer registers an empty `ApplicationStopping` callback; disposal behavior is owned by one clear mechanism; and unit coverage proves disposal is still idempotent and still disposes all cached `NpgsqlDataSource` instances.
steps-to-verify: Update `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/PostgresqlDataSourceCacheTests.cs` to keep coverage for builder settings, disposal of cached data sources, and repeated `Dispose()` calls after the lifetime-registration cleanup.
If application-lifetime shutdown disposal is retained, add an explicit test proving the callback triggers real cache cleanup rather than an empty registration.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter PostgresqlDataSourceCache`

Run: `dotnet csharpier format ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql/PostgresqlDataSourceCache.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/PostgresqlDataSourceCacheTests.cs`

description: Reduce the DMS-1108 branch/PR surface so the stable collection merge story is isolated from unrelated request-connection abstraction and DateTime referential-identity DDL parity work. Acceptance: the final DMS-1108 change set contains only files needed for stable collection merge plans, their serialization/tests, and the associated design-doc updates; unrelated changes in `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/IRequestConnectionProvider.cs`, `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Configuration/DmsInstanceRequestConnectionProvider.cs`, `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql/PostgresqlRequestDbConnectionProvider.cs`, `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql/PostgresqlDataSourceCache.cs`, and `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl/RelationalModelDdlEmitter.cs` are removed from this branch/PR or explicitly justified as hard dependencies in the story and PR description.
steps-to-verify: Review the branch diff against `origin/main` and confirm the remaining DMS-1108 files map directly to the story in `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/15-plan-compilation/04b-stable-collection-merge-plans.md`.
Verify the DMS-1108 branch no longer contains unrelated file changes with: `git diff --name-only $(git merge-base HEAD origin/main)..HEAD`.
If any unrelated-looking file must remain, document the dependency in the story/PR notes and ensure the explanation names the exact file and why DMS-1108 cannot ship without it.

description: Move the stable collection-merge invariant checks from `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/WritePlanCompiler.cs` into the public contract in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/Plans/WritePlanContracts.cs`. Acceptance: constructing `TableWritePlan` fails when `CollectionMergePlan` is present without `CollectionKeyPreallocationPlan`; constructing `TableWritePlan` fails when `CollectionMergePlan.StableRowIdentityBindingIndex` does not match `CollectionKeyPreallocationPlan.BindingIndex`; constructing `TableWritePlan` fails when the stable-row binding column name does not match `CollectionKeyPreallocationPlan.ColumnName`; valid collection plans compiled by `WritePlanCompiler` and decoded by `NormalizedPlanContractCodec` still construct successfully without needing a second compiler-only guard.
steps-to-verify: Add direct-constructor negative tests in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/ExternalPlanContractsTests.cs` covering missing `CollectionKeyPreallocationPlan`, mismatched binding index, and mismatched stable-row column name for collection table plans.

Update `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanContractCodecTests.cs` so mutated DTO payloads that violate those invariants fail during decode through the same public contract enforcement path.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "ExternalPlanContracts|NormalizedPlanContractCodec"`

description: Narrow collection matched-row update SQL in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/WritePlanCompiler.cs` so stable-row updates do not write structural locator columns. Acceptance: `EmitCollectionUpdateByStableRowIdentitySql` excludes root-scope and immediate-parent locator bindings from the `SET` list while still allowing writable semantic-identity, FK, scalar, key-unification, and `Ordinal` bindings required by the merge contract; compiled collection update SQL for top-level collections no longer sets root locator columns such as `School_DocumentId`; compiled collection update SQL for nested and collection-aligned extension tables no longer sets `ParentCollectionItemId` or `BaseCollectionItemId`; compare/no-op metadata remains unchanged and continues to exclude locator columns.
steps-to-verify: Update `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/Given_WritePlanCompiler_CollectionMerge.cs` to assert the revised `UPDATE` SQL omits structural locator columns for top-level, nested, and collection-aligned extension collection tables.

Keep or add assertions in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/RuntimeMappingSetCompilerTests.cs` proving collection plans still compile successfully and still publish the same semantic-identity and compare-order metadata after the SQL narrowing.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "CollectionMerge|RuntimeMappingSetCompiler"`

Run: `dotnet csharpier format ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/WritePlanCompiler.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/Given_WritePlanCompiler_CollectionMerge.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/RuntimeMappingSetCompilerTests.cs`

description: Reduce overlapping stable-collection-merge assertions so each test fixture owns one clear concern. Acceptance: detailed collection-merge contract shape assertions live in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/Given_WritePlanCompiler_CollectionMerge.cs`; `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/Given_WritePlanCompiler_DeleteByParentSql.cs` keeps only coverage that collections suppress `DeleteByParentSql` while non-collection 1:1 scopes retain it; `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/RuntimeMappingSetCompilerTests.cs` keeps only runtime-compilation smoke coverage for presence/absence of `CollectionMergePlan` rather than reasserting the full table-level semantic-identity contract; the remaining tests still cover top-level collections, nested collections, and collection-aligned extension collections without triplicate expectations.
steps-to-verify: Refactor `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/Given_WritePlanCompiler_DeleteByParentSql.cs` to remove duplicated semantic-identity and compare-order assertions while preserving coverage that collection tables do not emit `DeleteByParentSql`.

Trim `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/RuntimeMappingSetCompilerTests.cs` so it validates runtime compile success and contract presence/absence at the right level without repeating the detailed merge-shape checks from `Given_WritePlanCompiler_CollectionMerge.cs`.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "WritePlanCompiler_DeleteByParentSql|WritePlanCompiler_CollectionMerge|RuntimeMappingSetCompiler"`

description: Close the remaining stable-collection-merge contract gap in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/Plans/WritePlanContracts.cs` and `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanContractCodec.cs`. Acceptance: `TableWritePlan` rejects any plan that combines `CollectionMergePlan` with non-null `UpdateSql`; `TableWritePlan` rejects any plan that attaches `CollectionMergePlan` to a table whose `DbTableModel.IdentityMetadata.TableKind` is not `DbTableKind.Collection` or `DbTableKind.ExtensionCollection`; normalized DTO decode rejects the same invalid hybrids before they can be materialized from plain data; and new tests prove valid compiler-emitted collection plans still construct and round-trip successfully while hand-built or mutated invalid hybrids fail with clear diagnostics.
steps-to-verify: Add direct-constructor negative tests in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/ExternalPlanContractsTests.cs` covering `CollectionMergePlan` plus `UpdateSql`, and `CollectionMergePlan` attached to non-collection table kinds such as `RootExtension` or `CollectionExtensionScope`.

Add decode-path negative tests in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanContractCodecTests.cs` that mutate encoded plans to include `CollectionMergePlan` plus `UpdateSql`, and `CollectionMergePlan` on a non-collection `TableKind`, and assert decode fails through the same contract enforcement path.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "ExternalPlanContracts|NormalizedPlanContractCodec"`

description: Reduce drift risk in the test-only write-plan serialization stack introduced by DMS-1108. Acceptance: the write-plan contract shape for collection merge metadata is defined in one obvious place and reused consistently by `NormalizedPlanContractDtos`, `NormalizedPlanContractCodec`, `NormalizedPlanDtoJson`, and `MappingSetManifestJsonEmitter`; adding a future `TableWritePlan` collection field requires one shared contract update plus targeted formatter/emitter wiring instead of repeating field definitions across multiple ad hoc serializers; and existing deterministic JSON, manifest, and codec tests continue to pass without changing the emitted contract shape.
steps-to-verify: Refactor `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanContractDtos.cs`, `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanContractCodec.cs`, `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanDtoJson.cs`, and `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/MappingSetManifestJsonEmitter.cs` so collection-merge-field ownership is centralized and repeated field-plumbing logic is reduced without changing the serialized output.

Keep or add determinism assertions in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanContractDtosTests.cs`, `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanContractCodecTests.cs`, and `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/MappingSetManifestJsonEmitterTests.cs` proving the refactor preserves canonical JSON and manifest output byte-for-byte.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "NormalizedPlanContractDtos|NormalizedPlanContractCodec|MappingSetManifestJsonEmitter"`

description: Collapse duplicated stable-collection fixture setup into a shared builder so future contract changes touch one collection test model instead of several near-identical copies. Acceptance: the common `SchoolAddress` collection table model, column bindings, semantic-identity metadata, and collection-key-preallocation metadata used by `ExternalPlanContractsTests` and `NormalizedPlanContractCodecTests` come from one shared helper; both suites still express their own scenario-specific mutations without re-declaring the full fixture; and the refactor does not reduce negative-test coverage for invalid collection merge plans or codec mutations.
steps-to-verify: Extract a shared collection-plan fixture helper for the `SchoolAddress` collection model and bindings, then update `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/ExternalPlanContractsTests.cs` and `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanContractCodecTests.cs` to consume it.

Keep the existing negative coverage for invalid binding indexes, missing preallocation metadata, and alternate collection-key binding scenarios by mutating the shared fixture in each test instead of re-assembling the base table model inline.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "ExternalPlanContracts|NormalizedPlanContractCodec"`

description: Restore the DMS-1108 shared-versus-runtime compilation split by keeping `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/MappingSetCompiler.cs` and `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/WritePlanCompiler.cs` permissive for default/shared derived model sets while leaving `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RuntimeMappingSetCompiler.cs` strict at the executable runtime boundary. Acceptance: default/shared compilation against a `DerivedRelationalModelSet` produced with `RelationalModelSetPasses.CreateDefault()` can still materialize write plans for explicit collection tables whose `IdentityMetadata.SemanticIdentityBindings` are empty, so DDL/manifests/generic fixtures remain usable; runtime compilation continues to derive models with `CreateStrict()` and still fails deterministically for the same missing-semantic-identity fixture before executable merge plans are exposed; and the compiler does not silently invent fallback semantic keys from `Ordinal`, `CollectionItemId`, or parent-only locators.
steps-to-verify: Update `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/WritePlanCompiler.cs` and any required contract plumbing so shared/default compilation can represent permissive collection-table plans without tripping `CollectionMergePlan`'s non-empty semantic-identity invariant, while `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RuntimeMappingSetCompiler.cs` remains unchanged on `RelationalModelSetPasses.CreateStrict()`.

Add regression coverage in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/MappingSetCompilerTests.cs` or a targeted fixture test that compiles the negative missing-semantic-identity fixture through `new MappingSetCompiler().Compile(...)` and proves default/shared compilation stays permissive, and keep `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/RuntimeMappingSetCompilerTests.cs` asserting strict runtime compilation still throws the existing missing-semantic-identity diagnostic.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "MappingSetCompiler|RuntimeMappingSetCompiler|FocusedStableKeyRuntimePlanFixtureTests"`

Run: `dotnet csharpier format ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/MappingSetCompiler.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/WritePlanCompiler.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/MappingSetCompilerTests.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/RuntimeMappingSetCompilerTests.cs`

description: Correct collection compare/no-op metadata in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/WritePlanCompiler.cs` so `CollectionMergePlan.CompareBindingIndexesInOrder` reflects only stored/writable values that should participate in collection equality and guarded no-op comparison. Acceptance: compare-order derivation excludes storage-managed row identity and locator bindings such as `CollectionItemId`, `DocumentId`, `ParentCollectionItemId`, and `BaseCollectionItemId`, while still keeping stored/writable scalar, FK, canonical storage, synthetic presence, and recomputed `Ordinal` bindings in deterministic `ColumnBindings` order; compiler-emitted compare metadata matches the DMS-1108 design requirement that no-op comparison operates in stored/writable space rather than on internal row identity; and existing stable-row DML metadata continues to expose `StableRowIdentityBindingIndex` separately for matched-row update/delete execution.
steps-to-verify: Update `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/WritePlanCompiler.cs` so compare-order derivation filters out storage-managed stable row identity in addition to locator columns, and adjust any helper names or comments to make the intended compare-space classification explicit.

Update `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/Given_WritePlanCompiler_CollectionMerge.cs` and `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/ExternalPlanContractsTests.cs` so collection compare-order assertions no longer include `collectionItemId` and instead validate the remaining deterministic compare column order for top-level, nested, and extension collection plans.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "CollectionMerge|ExternalPlanContracts"`

Run: `dotnet csharpier format ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/WritePlanCompiler.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/Given_WritePlanCompiler_CollectionMerge.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/ExternalPlanContractsTests.cs`

description: Finish hardening the public stable-collection-merge contract in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/Plans/WritePlanContracts.cs` and the decode path in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanContractCodec.cs` so persisted collection tables cannot be materialized without executable merge metadata. Acceptance: a `TableWritePlan` whose `DbTableModel.IdentityMetadata.TableKind` is `DbTableKind.Collection` or `DbTableKind.ExtensionCollection` must expose a non-null `CollectionMergePlan` and must not be accepted when both `CollectionMergePlan` and `DeleteByParentSql` are absent; non-collection 1:1 scopes continue to allow the existing `InsertSql`/`UpdateSql`/`DeleteByParentSql` contract; normalized DTO decode rejects the same impossible collection shapes before they can become runtime-visible; and valid compiler-emitted collection plans still construct and round-trip successfully.
steps-to-verify: Add constructor-level validation in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/Plans/WritePlanContracts.cs` that requires `CollectionMergePlan` for `DbTableKind.Collection` and `DbTableKind.ExtensionCollection`, while preserving the existing contract for roots, root extensions, and collection-aligned 1:1 extension scopes.

Add negative tests in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/ExternalPlanContractsTests.cs` that attempt to construct collection table plans with `CollectionMergePlan: null` and assert they fail with clear diagnostics, and add matching mutation tests in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanContractCodecTests.cs` proving decode rejects the same serialized shape.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "ExternalPlanContracts|NormalizedPlanContractCodec"`

Run: `dotnet csharpier format ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/Plans/WritePlanContracts.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/ExternalPlanContractsTests.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/NormalizedPlanContractCodecTests.cs`
